# The Skitch Family Archive

This is a living collection of family history - stories, records, and memories gathered from letters, photographs, and the recollections of those who lived them.

The Skitch name arrived in Western Australia aboard the lives of Cornish emigrants. What followed was five generations of foresters and farmers, singers and servicemen, ordinary people living through extraordinary times. These documents are an attempt to preserve what might otherwise be forgotten.

The collection begins with Bob Skitch — known to his family as Junior — and his account of growing up in the 1930s and 40s in a Western Australia still shaped by the rhythms of the bush. More volumes will follow as they are transcribed and prepared.

## Documents

- [Junior](https://chris-skitch.github.io/family-history/rfs/1934-1945_junior.html) — 1934–1945
- [The Years Between](https://chris-skitch.github.io/family-history/rfs/1945-1955_the-years-between.html) — 1945–1955
- [In The Navy](https://chris-skitch.github.io/family-history/rfs/1953-1954_in-the-navy.html) — 1953–1954
- [The Army Years - Part 1 - The Young Soldier Surveyor](https://chris-skitch.github.io/family-history/rfs/1955-1962_the-army-years-part-1-the-young-soldier-surveyor.html) — 1955–1962
